## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Two-platform gauntlet exit. Operator-driven; failure triage is opus-grade — a failure here is a real cutover bug, not exploration.
  
  ## Goal
  
  `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh` passes on macOS and linux against the new layout.
  
  ## Work
  
  Run sequentially, never concurrent:
  
  - macOS branch off the ₣BK heat branch: `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`.
  - linux branch off the ₣BK heat branch: `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`.
  
  The gauntlet runs canonical-establish (depot regen) + qualification in one pass. Per-platform branches keep canonical-depot commits isolated.
  
  ### Triage on failure
  
  - **Grammar bug** (literal that escaped the sweep, wrong constant) — fix inline, re-run.
  - **Contract bug** (cwd assumption that escaped the audit, layout assumption in untested code path) — fix inline if mechanical, spin off a pace if architectural.
  - **Genuine architectural surprise** — stop, surface, decide. The cutover is wide enough that something subtle could surface only under the full gauntlet.
  
  ### Wrap criteria
  
  - Both platform runs green.
  - Any inline fixes are themselves committed as `jjx_record` entries against this pace.
  
  ## Sources
  
  - `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh` — pristine entry point
  - `Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbte_engine.sh` — gauntlet suite contents
  - `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrk_canonical.rs` — canonical-establish performs the depot regen
  - ₣A_/₢A_AAP — template pace pattern for two-platform acceptance

  ## Character
  
  Two-platform gauntlet exit. Operator-driven; failure triage is opus-grade — a failure here is a real cutover bug, not exploration.
  
  ## Goal
  
  `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh` passes on macOS and linux against the new layout.
  
  ## Work
  
  Run sequentially, never concurrent:
  
  - macOS branch off the ₣BK heat branch: `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`.
  - linux branch off the ₣BK heat branch: `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`.
  
  The gauntlet runs canonical-establish (depot regen) + qualification in one pass. Per-platform branches keep canonical-depot commits isolated.
  
  ### Triage on failure
  
  - **Grammar bug** (literal that escaped the sweep, wrong constant) — fix inline, re-run.
  - **Contract bug** (cwd assumption that escaped the audit, layout assumption in untested code path) — fix inline if mechanical, spin off a pace if architectural.
  - **Genuine architectural surprise** — stop, surface, decide. The cutover is wide enough that something subtle could surface only under the full gauntlet.
  
  ### Wrap criteria
  
  - Both platform runs green.
  - Any inline fixes are themselves committed as `jjx_record` entries against this pace.
  
  ## Sources
  
  - `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh` — pristine entry point
  - `Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbte_engine.sh` — gauntlet suite contents
  - `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrk_canonical.rs` — canonical-establish performs the depot regen
  - ₣A_/₢A_AAP — template pace pattern for two-platform acceptance

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BK
2	
3	## Shape
4	
5	Cutover of consumer-config + tabtarget path indirection per AAJ findings (₣A_/₢A_AAJ docket). Ten paces, hard cutover, branch-merge expected.
6	
7	The load-bearing mechanism is `tt/z-launcher.sh` — a trampoline that resolves moorings location, normalizes cwd to repo root, and dispatches to a named launcher. Every other change in this heat orbits that mechanism: the filesystem rename flips its hardcoded path literal; constant pools become downstream consumers of trampoline-established execution context; vocabulary sweeps make the new layout visible to the human reader.
8	
9	## Locked decisions
10	
11	- **Hard cutover**, no bridge period. Branch-merge atomic.
12	- **`rbcc_Constants.sh` is the RBK-side fact locale.** Existing file, extended — not a new file.
13	- **`bubc_constants.sh` is the BUK-side fact locale.** Established 2026-05-10, ready.
14	- **Trampoline hardcodes the moorings path literal.** Bash constant pools (RBCC, BUBC) are downstream consumers of trampoline-established execution context, not its inputs.
15	- **z-launcher.sh chdirs to repo root** before dispatching to the named launcher. Workbenches start with deterministic cwd regardless of user invocation directory.
16	- **Tabtarget dispatch sprue.** Tabtargets pass a minted moorings-launcher *sprue* to `tt/z-launcher.sh`, never a bare workbench-id — a bare id would conflate the hyphenated colophon universe with launcher dispatch. Form `{owner}ml_{launcher-id}`: RBK-authored launchers take `rbml_` (`rbml_rbw`, `rbml_rbtw`); the BUK launcher infrastructure that hosts every other kit takes `buml_` (`buml_buw`, `buml_jjw`, `buml_cmw`, `buml_vow`, `buml_vvw`, `buml_vslw`, `buml_apcw`, `buml_study`). The `rbml_`/`buml_` prefix is ownership-semantic, **not** a location selector — every launcher file co-locates in `rbmm_moorings/rbml_launchers/` after the rename (`.buk/` is deleted), so the trampoline recovers the launcher-id by stripping the `*ml_` prefix (`${1#*ml_}`) and dispatch stays a single literal across both families. The off-pattern `launcher_nolog.rbw_workbench.sh` takes its own sprue.
17	- **`rbm*_` prefix family**: `rbmm_` (umbrella), `rbml_` (launchers), `rbmn_` (nodes), `rbmu_` (users), `rbmv_` (vessels). `rbmn_` and `rbmu_` are the BURN/BURP regime pair — sibling consumer-authored profile subtrees under `.buk/`, defined side-by-side in BUBC; `rbmn_` joins the family rather than splitting off.
18	- **`buml_` prefix family**: BUK moorings launchers, parallel to `rbml_`. Registered as the dispatch-sprue namespace for every non-RBK launcher. A kit that later earns its own moorings migrates its sprue from `buml_` to `{kit}ml_`.
19	- **AAJ stays in ₣A_.** Nomination happened in agent conversation; AAJ's reslated form becomes "update A_ paddock with rbm*_ findings + verify ₣BK nominated + wrap."
20	
21	## Discipline
22	
23	- **Per-pace fast-suite smoke.** Each pace wrap runs `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh`; must pass before notch. 75 cases, no GCP deps, ~2-3 minutes. Catches breakage at the pace that caused it.
24	- **`git mv` for file moves** so history follows.
25	- **Single notch-commit per pace** where the work permits (`jjx_record` handles staging).
26	- **Parallel-internal dispatch** where the docket calls for it (paces 5 and 8): spawn agents on disjoint file subtrees within the same pace.
27	
28	## Out of scope
29	
30	- Extending moorings to other kits (CMK / JJK / VVK / VOK).
31	- Eliminating per-nameplate `compose.yml` files (they're consumer-authored).
32	- Redesigning launcher infrastructure beyond introducing `z-launcher.sh`.
33	- Per-customer migration of consumer-side trees outside this repo.
34	
35	## Sources
36	
37	- ₣A_ / ₢A_AAJ docket — design rationale, renames table, BCG codification commitment
38	- `Tools/buk/vov_veiled/BCG-BashConsoleGuide.md` — sections 21-30 (load-bearing complexity), 44-77 (CLI as module gateway), 171-181 (dispatch-provided BURD_*)
39	- `Tools/rbk/rbcc_Constants.sh` — RBK fact locale (extend, don't replace)
40	- `Tools/buk/bubc_constants.sh` — BUK fact locale (ready)

47	
48	## Goal
49	
50	`tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh` passes on macOS and linux against the new layout.
51

54	Run sequentially, never concurrent:
55	
56	- macOS branch off the ₣BK heat branch: `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`.
57	- linux branch off the ₣BK heat branch: `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`.
58	
59	The gauntlet runs canonical-establish (depot regen) + qualification in one pass. Per-platform branches keep canonical-depot commits isolated.
60	
61	### Triage on failure
62	
63	- **Grammar bug** (literal that escaped the sweep, wrong constant) — fix inline, re-run.
64	- **Contract bug** (cwd assumption that escaped the audit, layout assumption in untested code path) — fix inline if mechanical, spin off a pace if architectural.
65	- **Genuine architectural surprise** — stop, surface, decide. The cutover is wide enough that something subtle could surface only under the full gauntlet.

# jjezs_pace ₢BKAAJ

## Character

Two-platform gauntlet exit. Operator-driven; failure triage is opus-grade — a failure here is a real cutover bug, not exploration.

## Goal

`tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh` passes on macOS and linux against the new layout.

## Work

Run sequentially, never concurrent:

- macOS branch off the ₣BK heat branch: `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`.
- linux branch off the ₣BK heat branch: `tt/rbw-tP.QualifyPristine.sh`.

The gauntlet runs canonical-establish (depot regen) + qualification in one pass. Per-platform branches keep canonical-depot commits isolated.

### Triage on failure

- **Grammar bug** (literal that escaped the sweep, wrong constant) — fix inline, re-run.
- **Contract bug** (cwd assumption that escaped the audit, layout assumption in untested code path) — fix inline if mechanical, spin off a pace if architectural.
- **Genuine architectural surprise** — stop, surface, decide. The cutover is wide enough that something subtle could surface only under the full gauntlet.

### Wrap criteria

- Both platform runs green.
- Any inline fixes are themselves committed as `jjx_record` entries against this pace.

## Sources

- `Tools/rbk/rbq_Qualify.sh` — pristine entry point
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/rbte_engine.sh` — gauntlet suite contents
- `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrk_canonical.rs` — canonical-establish performs the depot regen
- ₣A_/₢A_AAP — template pace pattern for two-platform acceptance

148:  # Apply equivalent transform to rbrd.env.

158:      # Everything else passes through (comments, shebang, blanks)
163:  # Remove depot-scoped RBRA files (governor, director, retriever).

170:        buh_line "  Removed stale depot credential: ${z_rbra}"
175:  # Blank hallmark values in all vessel nameplates.
177:  # become stale after reset.  Blanking them causes the onboarding guide
191:    buh_line "  Blanked hallmarks: ${z_np}"
194:  # Blank depot-scoped fields in all vessel regime files.
211:      buh_line "  Blanked depot-scoped fields: ${z_vr}"
215:  buh_line "  Zero complete: ${z_rbrr}"
219:  # Auto-commit the in-tree mutations so post-zero state is captured as a single

241:    buh_line "  Marshal-zero state committed"

Everything else (RBRR/RBRD field blanking, hallmark/vessel blanking) is in-tree and auto-committed. This is the standard release-qualification reset — the cerebro run did exactly this ("Marshal Zero — release qualification reset"), and the gauntlet's canonical-establish regenerates the depot anyway, so the deleted credentials are tied to the prior depot.

You explicitly directed running MZ after the branch+push (both done), so I'll proceed. MZ has an interactive `"Proceed with marshal zero?"` confirmation — let me check how to satisfy it non-interactively.

content.text:
Found it — and it's **not** a cutover bug or code defect. The srjcl charge refused because of a **stale docker-network collision** left on this mac's daemon:

```
Nameplate 'srjcl' collision: 2 foreign compose network(s) match — clean up before charging:
  canrbhl-srjcl_enclave
  canrbhl-srjcl_transit
```

Note the prefix: `canrbhl-` (a `bhl` run), while this gauntlet's canonical prefix is `canrbhm-`. So these are **orphan networks from a prior run** on this machine that were never quenched — the charge's safety check correctly detected a foreign srjcl crucible and refused rather than stomp it. Everything up to here passed: enrollment 47, pristine 5, canonical 4, onboarding 8, regime-validation 27, regime-smoke 9, dockerfile-hygiene 9, hallmark 1, **tadmor 59, moriah 59** — all green. Only srjcl never got to run.

Let me confirm what those orphan networks hold before touching them.

315	######################################################################
316	# Charge Invariant — Network Collision Detection
317	
318	# Die if any compose-managed network with our nameplate exists from a foreign
319	# project. Catches pre-prefix-era residue (project=tadmor without RBRR
320	# prefix) and any other compose project whose name is the bare moniker or
321	# ends with "-{moniker}". Match is by labels — independent of how compose
322	# joined project + network into the network name.
323	zrbob_detect_nameplate_collision() {
324	  zrbob_sentinel
325	
326	  local -r z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_networks.txt"
327	  local -r z_networks_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_networks_stderr.txt"
328	  local -r z_inspect_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_inspect_"
329	
330	  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls \
331	    --filter 'label=com.docker.compose.project' \
332	    --filter 'label=com.docker.compose.network' \
333	    --format '{{.Name}}' \
334	    > "${z_networks_file}" 2>"${z_networks_stderr}" \
335	    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks for collision detection — see ${z_networks_stderr}"
336	
337	  local z_names=()
338	  local z_line=""
339	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
340	    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
341	    z_names+=("${z_line}")
342	  done < "${z_networks_file}"
343	
344	  local z_offenders=()
345	  local z_index=0
346	  local z_name=""
347	  local z_inspect_file=""
348	  local z_inspect_stderr=""
349	  local z_labels=""
350	  local z_net_label=""
351	  local z_proj_label=""
352	
353	  if (( ${#z_names[@]} )); then
354	    for z_name in "${z_names[@]}"; do
355	      z_inspect_file="${z_inspect_prefix}${z_index}.txt"
356	      z_inspect_stderr="${z_inspect_prefix}${z_index}_stderr.txt"
357	      z_index=$((z_index + 1))
358	
359	      ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network inspect "${z_name}" \
360	        --format '{{index .Labels "com.docker.compose.network"}}|{{index .Labels "com.docker.compose.project"}}' \
361	        > "${z_inspect_file}" 2>"${z_inspect_stderr}" \
362	        || continue
363	
364	      z_labels=$(<"${z_inspect_file}")
365	      z_net_label="${z_labels%%|*}"
366	      z_proj_label="${z_labels#*|}"
367	
368	      # Network must be enclave or transit
369	      test "${z_net_label}" = "enclave" || test "${z_net_label}" = "transit" \
370	        || continue
371	
372	      # Skip our own current project; compose-down already removed it (defensive)
373	      test "${z_proj_label}" != "${ZRBOB_PROJECT}" || continue
374	
375	      # Match: bare moniker, or any prefix ending in "-{moniker}"
376	      if test "${z_proj_label}" = "${RBRN_MONIKER}"; then
377	        z_offenders+=("${z_name}")
378	      elif test "${z_proj_label}" != "${z_proj_label%-"${RBRN_MONIKER}"}"; then
379	        z_offenders+=("${z_name}")
380	      fi
381	    done
382	  fi
383	
384	  test "${#z_offenders[@]}" -gt 0 || return 0
385	
386	  local z_msg="Nameplate '${RBRN_MONIKER}' collision: ${#z_offenders[@]} foreign compose network(s) match — clean up before charging:"
387	  local z_offender=""
388	  for z_offender in "${z_offenders[@]}"; do
389	    z_msg="${z_msg}"$'\n'"  docker rm \$(docker ps -aq --filter network=${z_offender}) 2>/dev/null; docker network rm ${z_offender}"
390	  done
391	  buc_die "${z_msg}"
392	}
393	
394	# Die if another Docker network already occupies this nameplate's subnet.
395	# Catches non-compose networks (manual `docker network create`) that the
396	# label-based scan above can't see. Our own network is excluded.
397	zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict() {
398	  zrbob_sentinel
399	
400	  local z_target_subnet="${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
401	  local z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_networks.txt"
402	  local z_subnet_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_subnet.txt"
403	
404	  # List all Docker network names
405	  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls --format '{{.Name}}' > "${z_networks_file}" \
406	    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks"
407	
408	  # Load network names (load-then-iterate per BCG)
409	  local z_names=()
410	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
411	    z_names+=("${z_line}")
412	  done < "${z_networks_file}"
413	
414	  # Inspect each network's subnet
415	  local z_name=""
416	  for z_name in "${z_names[@]}"; do
417	    # Skip our own compose network — compose down already handled it
418	    test "${z_name}" != "${ZRBOB_NETWORK}" || continue
419	
420	    ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network inspect "${z_name}" \
421	      --format '{{range .IPAM.Config}}{{.Subnet}}{{end}}' \
422	      > "${z_subnet_file}" 2>/dev/null || continue
423	
424	    local z_subnet=""
425	    z_subnet=$(<"${z_subnet_file}")
426	
427	    if test "${z_subnet}" = "${z_target_subnet}"; then
428	      buc_die "Subnet ${z_target_subnet} already claimed by stale network '${z_name}' — clean up manually: docker rm \$(docker ps -aq --filter network=${z_name}) 2>/dev/null; docker network rm ${z_name}"
429	    fi
430	  done
431	}
432	
433	######################################################################
434	# Public API
435	
436	# Start the crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle) via compose
437	# Requires: RBOB kindled (which requires RBRN and RBRR)
438	rbob_charge() {
439	  zrbob_sentinel
440	
441	  buc_step "Starting crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
442	
443	  # Gate: nameplate must have no uncommitted changes
444	  if ! git diff --quiet -- "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" 2>/dev/null; then
445	    buc_die "Nameplate has uncommitted changes: ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN} — commit before charging"
446	  fi
447	
448	  # Cross-nameplate validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
449	  rbrn_preflight
450	
451	  # Preflight: ensure all hallmark arks exist locally. Vouch presence is the
452	  # hallmark-level signal — if vouch is missing, the full hallmark is treated
453	  # as not-yet-summoned and all three arks are pulled together. Image-only
454	  # auto-summon below is defense-in-depth for partial-state cases.
455	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
456	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"; then
457	      buc_die "Kludge sentry hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
458	    fi
459	    buc_warn "Sentry vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}"
460	    zrbob_summon_full_hallmark "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
461	  fi
462	
463	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
464	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"; then
465	      buc_die "Kludge bottle hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
466	    fi
467	    buc_warn "Bottle vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}"
468	    zrbob_summon_full_hallmark "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
469	  fi
470	
471	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
472	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"; then
473	      buc_die "Kludge sentry hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
474	    fi
475	    buc_warn "Sentry image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
476	    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
477	  fi
478	
479	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
480	    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"; then
481	      buc_die "Kludge bottle hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
482	    fi
483	    buc_warn "Bottle image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
484	    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
485	  fi
486	
487	  # Tear down any prior state (tolerates missing project)
488	  buc_step "Cleaning up any prior state (quenching any preexisting can take a couple of minutes)"
489	  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true
490	
491	  # Charge invariant: at most one crucible per nameplate per system. The
492	  # collision scan refuses on any foreign compose project whose network
493	  # bears our moniker (catches pre-prefix-era residue independent of the
494	  # current run's project name); the subnet scan catches non-compose
495	  # networks that bypass label-based detection.
496	  zrbob_detect_nameplate_collision
497	  zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict
498	
499	  # Start services via compose (--profile sessile includes bottle)

######################################################################
# Charge Invariant — Network Collision Detection

# Die if any compose-managed network with our nameplate exists from a foreign
# project. Catches pre-prefix-era residue (project=tadmor without RBRR
# prefix) and any other compose project whose name is the bare moniker or
# ends with "-{moniker}". Match is by labels — independent of how compose
# joined project + network into the network name.
zrbob_detect_nameplate_collision() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  local -r z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_networks.txt"
  local -r z_networks_stderr="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_networks_stderr.txt"
  local -r z_inspect_prefix="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_collision_inspect_"

  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls \
    --filter 'label=com.docker.compose.project' \
    --filter 'label=com.docker.compose.network' \
    --format '{{.Name}}' \
    > "${z_networks_file}" 2>"${z_networks_stderr}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks for collision detection — see ${z_networks_stderr}"

  local z_names=()
  local z_line=""
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    test -n "${z_line}" || continue
    z_names+=("${z_line}")
  done < "${z_networks_file}"

  local z_offenders=()
  local z_index=0
  local z_name=""
  local z_inspect_file=""
  local z_inspect_stderr=""
  local z_labels=""
  local z_net_label=""
  local z_proj_label=""

  if (( ${#z_names[@]} )); then
    for z_name in "${z_names[@]}"; do
      z_inspect_file="${z_inspect_prefix}${z_index}.txt"
      z_inspect_stderr="${z_inspect_prefix}${z_index}_stderr.txt"
      z_index=$((z_index + 1))

      ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network inspect "${z_name}" \
        --format '{{index .Labels "com.docker.compose.network"}}|{{index .Labels "com.docker.compose.project"}}' \
        > "${z_inspect_file}" 2>"${z_inspect_stderr}" \
        || continue

      z_labels=$(<"${z_inspect_file}")
      z_net_label="${z_labels%%|*}"
      z_proj_label="${z_labels#*|}"

      # Network must be enclave or transit
      test "${z_net_label}" = "enclave" || test "${z_net_label}" = "transit" \
        || continue

      # Skip our own current project; compose-down already removed it (defensive)
      test "${z_proj_label}" != "${ZRBOB_PROJECT}" || continue

      # Match: bare moniker, or any prefix ending in "-{moniker}"
      if test "${z_proj_label}" = "${RBRN_MONIKER}"; then
        z_offenders+=("${z_name}")
      elif test "${z_proj_label}" != "${z_proj_label%-"${RBRN_MONIKER}"}"; then
        z_offenders+=("${z_name}")
      fi
    done
  fi

  test "${#z_offenders[@]}" -gt 0 || return 0

  local z_msg="Nameplate '${RBRN_MONIKER}' collision: ${#z_offenders[@]} foreign compose network(s) match — clean up before charging:"
  local z_offender=""
  for z_offender in "${z_offenders[@]}"; do
    z_msg="${z_msg}"$'\n'"  docker rm \$(docker ps -aq --filter network=${z_offender}) 2>/dev/null; docker network rm ${z_offender}"
  done
  buc_die "${z_msg}"
}

# Die if another Docker network already occupies this nameplate's subnet.
# Catches non-compose networks (manual `docker network create`) that the
# label-based scan above can't see. Our own network is excluded.
zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  local z_target_subnet="${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
  local z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_networks.txt"
  local z_subnet_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_subnet.txt"

  # List all Docker network names
  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls --format '{{.Name}}' > "${z_networks_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks"

  # Load network names (load-then-iterate per BCG)
  local z_names=()
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_names+=("${z_line}")
  done < "${z_networks_file}"

  # Inspect each network's subnet
  local z_name=""
  for z_name in "${z_names[@]}"; do
    # Skip our own compose network — compose down already handled it
    test "${z_name}" != "${ZRBOB_NETWORK}" || continue

    ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network inspect "${z_name}" \
      --format '{{range .IPAM.Config}}{{.Subnet}}{{end}}' \
      > "${z_subnet_file}" 2>/dev/null || continue

    local z_subnet=""
    z_subnet=$(<"${z_subnet_file}")

    if test "${z_subnet}" = "${z_target_subnet}"; then
      buc_die "Subnet ${z_target_subnet} already claimed by stale network '${z_name}' — clean up manually: docker rm \$(docker ps -aq --filter network=${z_name}) 2>/dev/null; docker network rm ${z_name}"
    fi
  done
}

######################################################################
# Public API

# Start the crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle) via compose
# Requires: RBOB kindled (which requires RBRN and RBRR)
rbob_charge() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_step "Starting crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"

  # Gate: nameplate must have no uncommitted changes
  if ! git diff --quiet -- "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" 2>/dev/null; then
    buc_die "Nameplate has uncommitted changes: ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN} — commit before charging"
  fi

  # Cross-nameplate validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
  rbrn_preflight

  # Preflight: ensure all hallmark arks exist locally. Vouch presence is the
  # hallmark-level signal — if vouch is missing, the full hallmark is treated
  # as not-yet-summoned and all three arks are pulled together. Image-only
  # auto-summon below is defense-in-depth for partial-state cases.
  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"; then
      buc_die "Kludge sentry hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
    fi
    buc_warn "Sentry vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}"
    zrbob_summon_full_hallmark "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
  fi

  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"; then
      buc_die "Kludge bottle hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
    fi
    buc_warn "Bottle vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}"
    zrbob_summon_full_hallmark "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
  fi

  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"; then
      buc_die "Kludge sentry hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
    fi
    buc_warn "Sentry image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
  fi

  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if zrbob_hallmark_is_kludge "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"; then
      buc_die "Kludge bottle hallmark not built locally: ${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
    fi
    buc_warn "Bottle image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
  fi

  # Tear down any prior state (tolerates missing project)
  buc_step "Cleaning up any prior state (quenching any preexisting can take a couple of minutes)"
  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true

  # Charge invariant: at most one crucible per nameplate per system. The
  # collision scan refuses on any foreign compose project whose network
  # bears our moniker (catches pre-prefix-era residue independent of the
  # current run's project name); the subnet scan catches non-compose
  # networks that bypass label-based detection.
  zrbob_detect_nameplate_collision
  zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict

  # Start services via compose (--profile sessile includes bottle)
  # --wait blocks until all health checks pass (sentry → pentacle → bottle chain)
  buc_step "Starting services via compose"
  zrbob_compose --profile sessile up -d --wait

  buc_step "Crucible started: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"

  if test "${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}" -gt 0; then
    buc_step "Waiting ${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}s for bottle service readiness"
    sleep "${RBRN_BOTTLE_READINESS_DELAY_SEC}"
  fi

  if test -x "${ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK}"; then
    buc_step "Running post-charge hook: ${ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK}"
    "${ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK}" || buc_die "Post-charge hook failed: ${ZRBOB_POST_CHARGE_HOOK}"
  fi

content.text:
This wasn't a missed force-delete — it was a **deliberate refusal**, and your recollection is right but with one important boundary. Let me lay out exactly what `charge` does, because the distinction is the whole answer.

`rbob_charge` (Tools/rbk/rbob_bottle.sh:438) cleans up in two stages:

**Stage 1 — force-teardown of its *own* prior state** (line 489):
```
zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans
```
This is the "forcibly delete any prior resources" you remember. But compose `down` only removes resources belonging to the **current compose project name**, which is prefix-scoped — `canrbhm-srjcl` for this run. So it reliably reclaims a prior crucible that shares this run's prefix+moniker.

**Stage 2 — collision guard for *foreign* resources** (`zrbob_detect_nameplate_collision`, line 323, called at 496):
This scans every compose network bearing the moniker `srjcl` and, for any whose project label is **not** the current one (line 373), it **`buc_die`s and prints manual cleanup commands** (line 391). It deliberately does **not** auto-delete.

That's exactly what bit us: the orphans were `canrbhl-srjcl_*` (project `canest2bhl100009`), a different prefix than this run's `canrbhm-`. Stage 1 couldn't see them (wrong project name); Stage 2 saw them and refused on purpose.

**Why the refusal is intentional:** a foreign-prefix crucible could belong to a *live* depot, a different station, or another operator sharing the daemon. Silently `docker network rm`-ing it would be the dangerous behavior. So charge is conservative by design — reclaim your own, refuse to stomp a stranger's.

**The actual root cause** is upstream of charge: those orphans are `Exited (255)` containers from a gauntlet run ~5 days ago that was **killed/interrupted mid-srjcl**, so its quench never ran. Charge behaved correctly; the leak happened at the earlier run's teardown.

So there are two genuinely different defensive levers, and they're operator-policy calls:

questions.question: The orphan-network collision was charge correctly refusing to stomp foreign-prefix resources; the leak came from an earlier killed run whose quench never executed. How do you want to harden against it? (This is a candidate spook — I'd capture it as a pace, not fix it inline during acceptance.)

options.label: Gauntlet-scoped orphan reaper
options.description: A preamble step in the test harness (or a standalone tabtarget) that sweeps stale crucible networks/containers for the nameplates it's about to test, across ALL prefixes. The harness owns the daemon, so aggressive reclaim is safe there — unlike interactive charge. Targets the leak directly.

options.label: Force-reclaim flag on charge
options.description: An opt-in env (e.g. RBOB_FORCE_RECLAIM=1) that turns the collision detector's refusal into auto-cleanup. Theurge fixtures set it; interactive charge keeps the conservative refuse-and-print default. Smaller change, reuses existing detection.

options.label: Leave charge as-is
options.description: The guard worked as designed; the failure was a 5-day-old orphan, not a recurring defect. Manual cleanup-on-collision (charge already prints the exact commands) may be acceptable rather than adding machinery.

content.text:
Honest answer: it's **not** an inconsistency at the level it first appears, but you're sniffing at a real coarseness one layer down.

**Why it's actually one rule, not two.** The discriminator in both stages is *identity* = project name = prefix+moniker, and there's a hard invariant behind it: **one crucible per nameplate per prefix.** The subnet a crucible claims comes from the *nameplate* config (`RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP`), not the prefix — so `canrbhl-srjcl` and `canrbhm-srjcl` both want the *same* subnet and physically cannot coexist. Given that:

- Stage 1 (same project) — a crucible with my exact identity is *provably a stale prior-me*. There's nothing else it could be. Reclaiming it is just idempotent takeover, and it has to be force, because re-charging your own nameplate is the common workflow — making that refuse-and-wait would be hostile.
- Stage 2 (foreign project, same moniker) — a *different* identity is squatting the subnet I need. I can't coexist with it, but I also have no authority to assert it's mine. Refusing is the namespace boundary doing its job; auto-stomping would silently break the isolation guarantee that prefixing exists to provide.

So "force-delete mine / refuse on theirs" is a single principle — *act within your own identity, don't unilaterally evict a different one* — applied across an ownership boundary. That asymmetry is load-bearing.

**The coarseness you're actually feeling.** Neither stage checks *liveness*. Stage 1 will happily kill your own crucible even if it's running; Stage 2 refuses a foreign one even when it's `Exited (255) 5 days ago` and obviously dead. That's the genuine wart: the dimension that *should* govern "safe to reap?" is "is anything actually using it?", but the code uses "is it mine?" as a proxy. The two coincide for the common case and diverge exactly in the case that bit us — a foreign, long-dead orphan.

If you wanted to remove the apparent inconsistency rather than paper over it, the principled move is to make the discriminator **liveness, not ownership**: reap any crucible whose containers are all dead (no running PIDs) regardless of prefix, and refuse only on a foreign crucible that's *actually live*. That collapses the two standards into one — "reclaim dead, refuse live" — and it would have auto-cleared this orphan without ever weakening the protection against stomping a running foreign depot.

Does that match the inconsistency you were sensing — and is "reap-dead-regardless-of-prefix, refuse-only-live-foreign" the direction you'd want a hardening pace to take?

content.text:
You're right, and the simpler instinct is the correct one. Here's the punchline, briefly:

**The foreign distinction guards a state that can't exist.** A crucible's subnet comes from the *nameplate* (`RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP`), not the prefix. So `canrbhl-srjcl` and `canrbhm-srjcl` both want the *same* subnet — they can never be up at the same time on one daemon. That means "a live foreign srjcl I need to protect" is already an impossible configuration. The foreign branch wasn't protecting a real thing; its only observable effect was to *refuse* instead of *reclaim* when the leftover happened to carry a different prefix. That's purely the wart that bit us — no safety earned.

So drop the permutation entirely: **charge always reclaims the srjcl crucible slot, whoever last held it.** That's not new aggression — it's just Stage 1's existing force-takeover ("re-charging my nameplate idempotently takes over") extended to cover all prefixes. There's only ever one srjcl crucible, so any srjcl crucible present *is* the one I'm replacing. Own-vs-foreign stops being a question.

And on your text-parsing skepticism — also right. The current foreign check does string surgery (`does this project label end in -srjcl?`) to *infer* lineage. Don't infer. Match on a structural fact: **the subnet** srjcl is about to claim (deterministic from the nameplate). Anything sitting on that subnet is, by definition, occupying the slot — tear it down, no name-guessing about whether it's "analogous" to today's srjcl. The subnet collision check (`zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict`) already computes that exact value; it just currently dies instead of reclaiming.

So the whole thing collapses to one rule: *charge ensures its subnet is free by reclaiming whatever occupies it.* One path, no foreign/own branch, no label string-munging.

Want me to capture that as a hardening pace — "collapse charge's two-stage cleanup into a single subnet-keyed reclaim" — for ₣BK or a follow-on heat? I won't touch code mid-acceptance; just want it recorded while it's clear.
